subject: DATA PROCESSING
preamble: This examination syllabus is derived from the Senior Secondary School curriculum on Data Processing published by the NERDC. It is designed to test basic knowledge and skills acquisition in data processing. A conceptual approach was used in preparing the syllabus, considering areas that would encourage the development of entrepreneurial skills for everyday living. This syllabus is not a teaching syllabus. It is an examination syllabus. Teachers are therefore advised to use the national teaching syllabus for the teaching-learning process.
objectives: Test candidates' acquisition of basic skills of data processing and management.
Test candidates' level of competence in ICT applications that will promote the acquisition of entrepreneurial skills for everyday living in the global world.
Test candidates' knowledge in the application of ICT in facilitating business transaction and education.
Test candidates' preparedness for further studies in Data Processing and Management.

examination_scheme.summary: There will be two papers, Papers 1 and 2 both of which shall be taken as a composite paper at one sitting.

papers.title: Multiple Choice Objective Test
papers.details: Will consist of forty multiple-choice objective questions, all of which are to be answered in 1 hour for 40 marks.

papers.title: Essay and Practical Test
papers.details: Will consist of two sections: Sections A and B. Candidates will be required to answer five questions in all. Section A: will consist of four essay questions. Candidates will be required to answer any three in 1 hour for 30 marks. Section B: will be a test of practical work. It shall consist of two compulsory essay questions to be answered in 1 hour for 40 marks.

topics.title: History of Computers
details: ii. Concept of Number system in computing.
iii. Generations of Computer: first, second, third, fourth, fifth generations of computer.

topics.title: Data and Information
details: i. Definition of data and information.

topics.title: Classification of Computers
details: Classify computers by: Type, Size, Usage.

topics.title: Application of ICT in everyday life
details: i. Uses of ICT in everyday activities.
ii. Impact of ICT in the society.

topics.title: The art of Information processing
details: ii. Steps involved in Information Processing.

topics.title: Medium of Information Transmission
details: ii. Classification of means of transmission.

topics.title: Networking
details: i. Meaning of Networking, Internet and Intranet.

topics.title: Word Processing
details: iii. Examples of word Processing software.
iv. Starting, loading and exiting word processing.
v. Creating, saving and retrieving documents.

topics.title: Spreadsheet
details: v. Creating, saving and retrieving Spreadsheet files.

topics.title: Database Management System
details: v. Creating, saving and retrieving database files.

topics.title: Presentation Package
details: ii. Loading and exiting Presentation programs.
iii. Creating, saving and retrieving presentation files.

topics.title: Graphic Packages
details: ii. Uses of Graphic software packages.

syllabus_content.section_title: COMPUTER MAINTENANCE, ETHICS AND HUMAN ISSUES

topics.title: Maintenance of Computer
details: ii. Battery Charging and replacement for portable systems and UPS.
vi. Computer crash and data recovery.

topics.title: Safety Measures
details: ii. Positioning of the monitor, keyboard, CPU, mouse and any other peripheral devices.
iv. Maintaining a dust free environment.
v. Keep liquid away from the computer room.

topics.title: Career opportunities in Data Processing
details: ii. Qualities of a good data processing professional.

topics.title: Computer Virus
details: v. Virus prevention, detection and deletion.

topics.title: Relational Model
details: i. Database and table creation using application packages.
iii. Creation of forms, queries and report.

topics.title: Database Security
details: i. Concept of data security, access control and data encryption.
ii. Role of a database administrator.

topics.title: Parallel and Distributed databases
details: i. Basic concept of parallel and distributed database.
ii. Storing data in a distributed database (DBMS).

appendices.title: LIST OF FACILITIES AND MAJOR EQUIPMENT/MATERIALS REQUIRED
details: (1) Pictures/Charts showing different computing devices and other relevant information such as computers in a network - Quantity: 5
(2) Computer system with Graphical User Interface (GUI), internet ready system - Quantity: 5
(3) Standard software packages such as Open Office, Microsoft office etc. (Latest version is desirable) - Quantity: 1
(4) Multimedia systems and applications such as projector, speakers - Quantity: 1
(5) Graphic packages such as CorelDraw - Quantity: 1
